# Oracle Class Feats (Levels 12-20)

Source: Archives of Nethys (https://2e.aonprd.com)
Includes: Player Core 2, Player Core, Dark Archives (Remastered), Book of the Dead, Knights of Lastwall, Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain
Where remastered versions exist, those are used (Player Core 2 / Dark Archives Remastered preferred).

---

## Level 12 Feats

### Domain Fluency &mdash; Feat 12

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 141
- **Prerequisites:** Domain Acumen
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6074

You command a deep understanding of the domains related to your mystery. Choose one of the domains associated with your mystery for which you have an initial domain spell. You gain an advanced domain spell from that domain, which you cast as a revelation spell.

---

### Epiphany at the Crossroads &mdash; Feat 12

- **Actions:** Free Action
- **Traits:** Divine, Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 142
- **Frequency:** once per day
- **Trigger:** Your turn begins.
- **Requirements:** You are unconscious and have the dying condition.
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6075

The crossroads between life and death is a place that can reveal many secrets. You gain the effects of an augury spell in a strange, near-death vision. Empowered by the realization, you then lose the dying condition (becoming wounded 1 or increasing your wounded value by 1, as normal), regain a number of Hit Points equal to twice your level, and can Stand.

---

### Greater Revelation &mdash; Feat 12

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 142
- **Prerequisites:** initial revelation spell
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6076

You unlock deeper revelations hidden within your mystery. You gain your mystery's greater revelation spell.

*Leads to... Paradoxical Mystery*

---

### Magic Sense &mdash; Feat 12

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Arcane, Detection, Magus, Oracle, Sorcerer, Wizard
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 204; Player Core 2 pg. 142
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 5044

You have a literal sixth sense for ambient magic in your vicinity. You can sense the presence of magic auras as though you were always using a 1st-rank detect magic spell. This detects magic in your field of vision only. When you Seek, you gain the benefits of a 3rd-rank detect magic spell on things you see (in addition to the normal benefits of Seeking). You can turn this sense off and on with a free action at the start or the end of your turn.

---

### Necromancer's Visage &mdash; Feat 12

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Cleric, Necromancy, Oracle, Wizard
- **Source:** Book of the Dead pg. 33
- **Prerequisites:** ability to create or control undead; cleric with a negative font, oracle of bones, or necromancer wizard
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 3484

Necromantic energy demonstrates your power over undead, creating a demeanor that commands their fear and respect. Undead creatures can immediately sense your Necromancer's Visage and living creatures can sense it with a successful Perception check against your spell DC. Mindless undead won't attack you unless directed to by their controller or unless you attack them first. If you take a hostile action against an undead creature or its allies, it's temporarily immune to this feat's effects for 24 hours. The living find your appearance unsettling; you gain a +1 status bonus to Intimidation checks against living creatures, and you take a –1 status penalty to Diplomacy checks against them.

---

## Level 14 Feats

### Forestall Curse &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Free Action
- **Traits:** Concentrate, Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 142
- **Frequency:** once per day
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6077

You've learned to hold back your curse. If the next action you use is a cursebound ability, your cursebound value doesn't increase.

---

### Lighter than Air &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Divine, Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 142
- **Prerequisites:** Water Walker
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6078

Your mysterious steps become even lighter, transcending the mortal world altogether. When you have the cursebound condition, you gain the effects of fly. If you are cursebound 3 or greater, you gain a +10-foot status bonus to your fly Speed.

---

### Mysterious Repertoire &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 142
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6079

Your mystery holds unknowable depths of magic not always associated with the divine. You can have one spell in your spell repertoire not on the divine spell list, in addition to spells you've added to your spell list from abilities like divine access. You cast that spell as a divine spell. You can swap which spell you add and from which tradition as you could any other oracle spell, but you can't use this feat to have more than one spell from another tradition in your spell repertoire at the same time.

---

### Purifying Breeze &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Single Action
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Cleric, Oracle, Sorcerer
- **Source:** Knights of Lastwall pg. 85
- **Prerequisites:** divine spells
- **Access:** Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.
- **Requirements:** Your most recent action was to cast a non-cantrip spell with the healing trait.
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 3643

You transfer the divine essence of your magic to the air around you, cleansing it of toxins. Attempt a counteract check against each airborne disease or poison effect in a 15-foot radius around you. Regardless of your success or failure, until the beginning of your next turn, all creatures in the purified area gain a +1 status bonus to Fortitude saving throws.

---

### Revelation's Focus &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6080

The further extents of your mystery provide an endless wellspring of magic. You increase the maximum number of Focus Points in your focus pool from 3 to 4. As a revelation spell has a focus cost of 1 Focus Point, this lets you use a revelation spell up to four times before needing to Refocus (as opposed to the normal maximum of three times).

---

### Sacral Monarch &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Champion, Oracle
- **Source:** Dark Archives (Remastered) pg. 177
- **Prerequisites:** Sacral Lord; master in Occultism or Religion
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 8479

You have come into your full powers and are truly a monarch of your realm, by your own definition at least. While in your territory, you can cast banishment and inevitable disaster as innate divine spells, each once per day.

---

### Sepulchral Sublimation &mdash; Feat 14

- **Actions:** Single Action
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Cleric, Concentrate, Metamagic, Necromancy, Oracle, Wizard
- **Source:** Book of the Dead pg. 33
- **Prerequisites:** ability to create or control undead; cleric with a negative font, oracle of bones, or necromancer wizard
- **Requirements:** You have at least one undead minion that you permanently control.
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 3485

You repurpose the negative energy that flows through your minions at a moment's notice. Instantly destroy one undead minion within 60 feet that is permanently under your control. If your next action is to cast a necromancy spell from a spell slot, you don't expend the slot. The spell's level must be half the destroyed minion's level or lower and the spell can't have a duration.

**Special** This feat has the trait matching your spellcasting tradition.

---

## Level 16 Feats

### Conduit of Void and Vitality &mdash; Feat 16

- **Actions:** Two Actions
- **Traits:** Cursebound, Divine, Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **Prerequisites:** any oracle mystery
- **Requirements:** You have heal or harm as a signature spell and an available spell slot to cast it with.
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6081

You use the unstable energy of your curse to manipulate the most basic of divine magic. You cast a 3-action heal or harm, expending a spell slot as normal. If the spell heals one or more creatures, then one creature healed by this spell regains a number of additional Hit Points equal to 1d8 &times; your cursebound value; if the spell damages one or more creatures, then one creature harmed by this spell takes additional damage equal to 1d8 &times; your cursebound value.

---

### Diverse Mystery &mdash; Feat 16

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6082

You have broadened your understanding of the divine and can tap into the wonders of a different mystery. Select one revelation spell from a mystery other than your own. You can choose only an initial revelation spell or an advanced revelation spell. This spell gains the cursebound trait for you, and when you cast it, you gain the cursebound 1 effects of its mystery in addition to your normal curse effects. Any ability that lets you use a cursebound ability without increasing the severity of your curse also prevents you from gaining this additional curse effect. You can't cast your chosen revelation spell if any of the following conditions are true: its mystery's curse effects directly conflict with or negate the effects of your own mystery's curse, the curse would have no effect on you (for example, removing an ability from your original mystery that you lack), or if either of these criteria would be met once you finish Casting the Spell.

---

### Portentous Spell &mdash; Feat 16

- **Actions:** Single Action
- **Traits:** Manipulate, Mental, Oracle, Spellshape, Visual
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6083

Your spellcasting is rife with strange lights, mild fumes, and other captivating effects that befuddle your foes. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell, any creature that attempts to use a reaction triggered by your Cast a Spell activity takes a &ndash;2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and skill checks rolled as part of the reaction. In addition, if the spell includes a spell attack or requires a saving throw, creatures you hit or that fail their saves are fascinated with you until the start of your next turn.

---

## Level 18 Feats

### Blaze of Revelation &mdash; Feat 18

- **Actions:** Free Action
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **Requirements:** You are cursebound 4.
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6084

Your mind and body can, for a short time and with great peril, surpass the final limits of your curse. For 1 minute, you shed torrential flames, become surrounded by spirits, or otherwise overflow with divine power in a way that suits your mystery. On each of your turns during that time, you can cast one revelation spell without spending Focus Points. At the end of the minute, the durations of any revelation spells you cast during that time end, and you must attempt a DC 40 Fortitude save.

**Critical Success** You aren't otherwise affected.
**Success** You are drained 2 and can't reduce or remove this condition until your next preparations.
**Failure** You are drained 4 and can't reduce or remove this condition until your next preparations.
**Critical Failure** You die.

---

### Divine Effusion &mdash; Feat 18

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6085

The power of your mystery surges through you like a font of divine energy, even when your own magic dwindles. Twice per day, you can cast a spell after you've run out of spell slots of the appropriate spell rank; the two spells you cast with this feat must be of different spell ranks.

---

## Level 20 Feats

### Mystery Conduit &mdash; Feat 20

- **Actions:** Free Action
- **Traits:** Cursebound, Oracle, Spellshape
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6086

The power of your mystery enables you access to myriad magic. If your next action is to Cast a Spell of 5th rank or lower that has no duration, you do not expend a spell slot.

---

### Oracular Providence &mdash; Feat 20

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **Prerequisites:** oracular clarity
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6087

Your mystery grants you access to deep reserves of truly miraculous divine power. You gain an additional 10th-rank spell slot.

---

### Paradoxical Mystery &mdash; Feat 20

- **Actions:** Passive
- **Traits:** Oracle
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 143
- **Prerequisites:** Greater Revelation
- **PFS:** Standard
- **AoN ID:** 6088

The truest depths of your divine mystery are fathomless and contradictory, granting you changing powers that even you can barely begin to fathom. Each day during your daily preparations, choose one basic or advanced domain spell from any domain from Player Core, one of the domains granted by your mystery, another domain to which you have access, or any initial or advanced revelation spell from another mystery. You gain that spell as a revelation spell until your next daily preparations.

---

### Scapegoat Parallel Self &mdash; Feat 20

- **Actions:** Reaction
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Divine, Necromancy, Oracle
- **Source:** Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain pg. 76
- **Frequency:** once per day
- **Trigger:** Your turn starts or your oracular curse would advance.
- **PFS:** Limited
- **AoN ID:** 2753
- **Note:** Legacy content; may contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix. This is a capstone feat available upon entering the Glass Lighthouse or by meditating on the cushion in area H4.

Seeing Syndara superimpose creatures with similar metaphysical properties, you've realized a way to manipulate the effects of your oracular curse. You immediately change your curse to its minor, moderate, or major stage, or back to its mild form, exchanging the curse effect with that of a parallel version of yourself in another reality.
